spec_match.rationale: Exercises the most-cited motivation for the relaxation: mixed-case SVG / MathML element names (linearGradient, mtable, foreignObject) and colon-namespaced attributes (xlink:href) — the canonical pre-v143 InvalidCharacterError zone.

interactive.rationale: Four sample pills load editable source markup that drives a live parser-vs-rebuild roundtrip, with serialised output, normalised diff verdict, and a visual rendered-pair (parser-built vs JS-rebuilt) side-by-side.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'Pick an SVG or MathML snippet, parse it, then rebuild the whole tree with createElementNS / setAttributeNS. Both forms now render identically.' — page delivers that with the visual pair.

edge_cases.rationale: Whitespace-normalised string diff masks attribute order, which can produce a green verdict for trees that serialise differently. No probe of namespace-prefixed attributes whose namespace differs from the element's (e.g. xml:lang inside SVG).

references.rationale: See-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6278918763708416 and the DOM namespaces spec.

design_system.rationale: All colours from CSS variables; ok/bad diffs use --accent-emerald / --accent-rose, render panels use --bg-paper. AA contrast holds.

openQuestions.title: Use a structural diff instead of normalised-string compare
openQuestions.detail: The diff function serialises both trees, collapses whitespace, then string-equals. SVG element attribute order is not stable across createElementNS rebuilds, so the page will sometimes flag a perfectly correct rebuild as 'different'. A walk-both-trees-in-parallel comparator (compare namespaceURI, localName, attributes-as-set, child counts) would give an honest verdict.

openQuestions.title: Add an xml: / xlink: attribute on a non-SVG element
openQuestions.detail: The relaxation specifically helps templating frameworks that need to set namespaced attributes on arbitrary elements. A fifth sample that puts xml:lang on an HTML div would broaden the spec coverage beyond just SVG and MathML roots.

openQuestions.title: Surface the per-attribute failure path
openQuestions.detail: rebuild() wraps setAttributeNS in try/catch and appends a comment node on failure — but those comments never get surfaced in the page UI. A small 'attributes that fell back to comments' list under the verdict would teach what is still broken even after v143.

openQuestions.title: Add a foreignObject-with-Unicode-tag sample
openQuestions.detail: foreignObject is the seam where SVG namespace ends and XHTML namespace begins. A sample with `<foreignObject><café-card>x</café-card></foreignObject>` would test that both relaxations cooperate: namespaced element name AND Unicode HTML tag inside it.

summary: Strong canonical roundtrip demo with rendered visual comparison. Two notable fixes: replace the brittle normalised-string diff with a structural comparator, and add a foreignObject sample that crosses the SVG ↔ HTML namespace boundary.
